summary: Joel shares a walkability scoring website created by his friend Mike Mathieu. He compares the walkability scores of Microsoft's campus (31), Google's campus (34), and Fog Creek's New York City office (98 out of 100). The post is a lighthearted plug for Fog Creek's urban office location, concluding with a tongue-in-cheek recruitment pitch.
key_points: Suburban tech campuses like Microsoft and Google score poorly on walkability (31 and 34 respectively)
Fog Creek's Manhattan office scores 98 out of 100 for walkability
Urban office locations offer lifestyle advantages over suburban corporate campuses
money_quotes: Conclusion: Ditch your car, work at Fog Creek.
key_insight: Fog Creek's urban New York location offers a walkable lifestyle advantage over the suburban campuses of tech giants like Microsoft and Google.
